name: reducibility-idioms
description: Per-language idiom-map for the reducibility reviewer. Maps each closed-catalog code (D/A/X/C/S/M/P/T) to recognizable idioms the reviewer should propose, plus a long-but-coherent exemption note (G1) per code/language. Reviewer context only — not runtime-parsed by product code. SSoT prose lives in shared/reducibility-catalog.md.

idioms: 3+ near-identical blocks → one named helper
copy-pasted error-handling → a shared wrapper/guard
D.long_but_coherent: Two copies, or copies in two genuinely-decoupled domains, are not a D-finding (G5).

idioms: single-caller wrapper / factory / indirection → inline it
options-flag with one variant → drop the flag
A.long_but_coherent: An abstraction with 2+ real callers or a documented extension point is justified, not needless.

idioms: unreachable branch / unused symbol → delete
commented-out code block → delete (history is in git)
X.long_but_coherent: A deliberately-unused public API hook with a why-comment is not dead code.

idioms: deep nesting → guard clause + early return
long if/elif ladder dispatching on a key → table/map dispatch
C.long_but_coherent: Sequential straight-line steps that each read clearly are coherent, not verbose (G2).

idioms: same positional-arg bag threaded through many calls → one record/type
parallel arrays kept in sync by hand → one array of records
S.long_but_coherent: A one-off two-field tuple at a single call site does not need a named type.

idioms: comment paraphrasing the next line → delete
redundant section banners around self-evident code → delete
M.long_but_coherent: Why-comments (rationale, gotchas, links to issues/ADRs) are load-bearing — never an M-finding.

idioms: new dependency for a few lines the stdlib already covers → drop it
heavyweight lib used for one trivial helper → inline the helper
P.long_but_coherent: A dependency that replaces a large, error-prone hand-roll is a net win, not footprint.

idioms: copy-pasted test bodies differing only in data → parametrize / table-driven
repeated setup across tests → a fixture
T.long_but_coherent: Parametrizing must keep every asserted case; never collapse cases to shrink the file (G3).

idioms: repeated try/except subprocess blocks → one helper (see group_g._git_* pattern)
duplicated dict/JSON parsing → a shared loader returning a typed value
D.long_but_coherent: Per-check duplicated git plumbing kept for crash-isolation is justified (G5).

idioms: class with one method + __init__ → a module-level function
single-subclass ABC / Protocol → drop the base
A.long_but_coherent: Dataclasses, Protocols with multiple implementers, and ABCs with real subclasses are justified.

idioms: unused import / param flagged by ruff F401/F841 → delete
`if False:` / unreachable after return → delete
X.long_but_coherent: `from __future__ import annotations` and re-exports in __init__ are not dead.

idioms: for-append loop → list/dict/set comprehension when it stays readable
nested if → early `return`/`continue`; `x if c else y` for trivial branches
C.long_but_coherent: A comprehension with multiple clauses that needs re-reading should stay a loop (G2).

idioms: functions passing the same 4+ kwargs around → a @dataclass(frozen=True)
dict-with-fixed-keys threaded through code → a dataclass or NamedTuple
S.long_but_coherent: A short-lived internal dict at one boundary does not need a class.

idioms: `# returns the result` over `return result` → delete
docstring restating the signature with no semantics → trim
M.long_but_coherent: Module/function docstrings explaining intent and edge cases stay.

idioms: `requests` for a single GET where `urllib`/an existing client works → drop
a date lib for one strftime → use `datetime`
P.long_but_coherent: PyYAML/`tomllib` for real config parsing is justified; don't hand-roll a parser.

idioms: N near-identical test funcs → `@pytest.mark.parametrize`
repeated arrange blocks → a `@pytest.fixture`
T.long_but_coherent: Distinct behaviors deserve distinct named tests even if setup looks similar.

idioms: repeated fetch/error-mapping → a shared client helper
duplicated zod/validation shapes → one exported schema
D.long_but_coherent: Server vs client copies that must not couple are justified duplication (G5).

idioms: single-use HOC / wrapper component → inline the markup
factory returning one concrete type → a plain function/const
A.long_but_coherent: A shared UI primitive with several consumers is justified, not needless.

idioms: unused export / import / prop flagged by tsc/oxlint → delete
dead feature-flag branch after rollout → delete
X.long_but_coherent: Barrel re-exports and public package entry points are not dead code.

idioms: nested ternaries / if-chains → early return or a lookup object
imperative map+push loop → `.map`/`.filter`/`.reduce` when readable
C.long_but_coherent: A `.reduce` that obscures intent should stay an explicit loop (G2).

idioms: many positional args → a single typed options object
stringly-typed unions repeated everywhere → one `type`/`enum`
S.long_but_coherent: A two-prop inline object literal at one call site needs no named interface.

idioms: `// set loading true` over `setLoading(true)` → delete
JSDoc duplicating the TS types with no extra info → trim
M.long_but_coherent: JSDoc adding `@deprecated`, examples, or rationale is load-bearing.

idioms: lodash for `Array.prototype` methods that exist natively → drop
a date lib for one format → `Intl.DateTimeFormat`
P.long_but_coherent: A focused, tree-shakeable dep replacing fragile hand-rolled logic is justified.

idioms: copy-pasted `it()` blocks differing only in input → `it.each`/`test.each`
repeated render setup → a test helper / `beforeEach`
T.long_but_coherent: Keep every asserted case when converting to `.each`; don't drop cases (G3).
